configuration-element.name: RuntimeState
configuration-element.description:
Runtime state for the Adaptive Snapshot Cleanup workflow. Holds the distributed mutex lock (runLock) that prevents more than one workflow instance from executing simultaneously.
This is the only configuration element required by this solution. All threshold and policy values are managed as workflow input parameters with defaults set on the workflow Inputs tab and on each schedule definition.
STUCK LOCK RECOVERY: If the workflow exits abnormally without releasing the lock, all subsequent scheduled runs will abort with MUTEX_ABORT. To recover: 1. Confirm no run is currently executing (check vRO Runs tab) 2. Set runLock attribute to empty string 3. Save — next run proceeds normally
INTENTIONAL DISABLE: Set runLock to any non-empty non-run-ID value such as "MAINTENANCE". All run attempts will abort at the mutex check and log MUTEX_ABORT. Clear to empty string to re-enable.

attribute.name: runLock
attribute.description: Active run ID while a workflow run is in progress (format: SCR-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS). Empty string when no run is active. Written by ST-02 on acquire. Cleared by ST-09 and EH on release. CRITICAL: initialise as empty string, never null.

attribute.name: lastRunId
attribute.description: Run ID of the most recently completed or aborted run. Informational only.

attribute.name: lastRunCompletedAt
attribute.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last run completion. Informational only.

attribute.name: lastRunOutcome
attribute.description: Outcome of the last run: SUCCESS | DRY_RUN_COMPLETE | COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS | COMPLETED_WITH_DEFERRALS | CLEAN_EXIT | MUTEX_ABORT | ERROR. Informational only.
